items.q: Is a tennis serve a closed or open skill?
items.correct: Closed - performed in a predictable, self-paced environment.
items.misconception: Open - because tennis is a fast-paced sport.
items.why: Students judge by the sport, not the moment. The serve happens at the server's pace with a stationary ball - closed. The return of serve is open. Skill classification depends on the SPECIFIC action's environment.

items.q: Which energy system fuels a marathon?
items.why: Students associate hard exercise with anaerobic systems. Long-duration steady-state work uses AEROBIC metabolism (oxygen + carbs/fats) - efficient but slower to ramp up. Anaerobic systems dominate brief intense efforts.

items.q: What does the 'S' in SPORT stand for?
items.correct: Specificity - training should match the demands of the sport.
items.why: Students guess fitness components. SPORT = Specificity, Progression, Overload, Reversibility, Tedium (or Time). Specificity means training matches what the athlete needs (sprinter doesn't train like a marathoner).
Correct answer: Look around for info. “Take a picture” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: First option may not work. “For dance” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How often (in seconds) might top players scan?
items.why: Exact answer: 5. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
